image-alt.plain: Some images are missing text alternatives, so screen reader users cannot tell what they show.

input-image-alt.plain: Some image buttons are missing text alternatives, so screen reader users cannot tell what they do.

area-alt.plain: Some clickable image-map areas are missing text alternatives.

object-alt.plain: Some embedded objects are missing text alternatives.

svg-img-alt.plain: Some SVG graphics are missing accessible names, so assistive technology cannot describe them.

role-img-alt.plain: Some elements presented as images are missing alternative text.

video-caption.plain: Some videos do not provide captions, excluding deaf and hard-of-hearing users from the content.

definition-list.plain: Some definition lists are not structured correctly, so their meaning is lost to assistive technology.

dlitem.plain: Some definition list items sit outside a proper definition list, breaking their structure for assistive technology.

list.plain: Some lists contain elements that do not belong there, which confuses screen readers announcing list structure.

listitem.plain: Some list items are not contained in a list, so screen readers cannot announce them as part of one.

td-headers-attr.plain: Some data table cells reference header cells incorrectly, making the table hard to understand with a screen reader.

th-has-data-cells.plain: Some table headers do not relate to any data cells, making table structure unclear to assistive technology.

autocomplete-valid.plain: Some form fields have invalid autocomplete attributes, preventing browsers and assistive tools from helping users fill them in.

color-contrast.plain: Some text does not have enough contrast against its background, making it hard to read for people with low vision.

meta-viewport.plain: The page blocks zooming on mobile devices, preventing people with low vision from enlarging text.

link-in-text-block.plain: Some links are distinguished from surrounding text by color alone, which colorblind users cannot perceive.

bypass.plain: There is no way to skip repeated navigation, forcing keyboard users to tab through every menu item on every page.

document-title.plain: Some pages are missing a descriptive title, so users cannot tell pages apart in tabs, bookmarks, or screen readers.

link-name.plain: Some links have no accessible name, so screen reader users hear only 'link' with no idea where it goes.

frame-title.plain: Some embedded frames are missing titles, so screen reader users cannot tell what content they contain.

button-name.plain: Some buttons have no accessible name, so screen reader users cannot tell what pressing them will do.

select-name.plain: Some dropdown menus have no accessible name, so screen reader users cannot tell what they choose.

aria-input-field-name.plain: Some custom input fields have no accessible name, so assistive technology cannot describe them.

aria-command-name.plain: Some custom buttons, links, or menu items have no accessible name.

aria-toggle-field-name.plain: Some custom checkboxes, switches, or radio buttons have no accessible name.

label.plain: Some form fields are missing labels, so users of assistive technology cannot tell what to enter.

aria-required-attr.plain: Some ARIA roles are missing attributes they require, so assistive technology receives incomplete information.

aria-valid-attr-value.plain: Some ARIA attributes have invalid values, which can mislead assistive technology about the state of the page.

aria-valid-attr.plain: Some ARIA attributes are misspelled or do not exist, so assistive technology cannot interpret them.

aria-allowed-attr.plain: Some elements use ARIA attributes that are not allowed for their role, producing unreliable assistive-technology behavior.

aria-hidden-focus.plain: Some elements hidden from assistive technology can still receive keyboard focus, leaving keyboard users stranded on invisible content.

nested-interactive.plain: Some interactive controls are nested inside each other, which assistive technology cannot operate reliably.

scrollable-region-focusable.plain: Some scrollable areas cannot be reached with the keyboard, hiding their content from keyboard-only users.

html-has-lang.plain: The page does not declare its language, so screen readers may read the content with the wrong pronunciation rules.

html-lang-valid.plain: The page declares an invalid language code, so screen readers may mispronounce the content.

valid-lang.plain: Some sections declare an invalid language code, so screen readers may mispronounce them.

form-field-multiple-labels.plain: Some form fields have multiple labels, so assistive technology may announce the wrong one.

meta-refresh.plain: Some pages refresh or redirect automatically on a timer, which users cannot pause or control.
